free_text: 232Th metal foil wrapped with a 0.025 mm thick super
pure aluminium foil.The size of each 232Th metal foil
was about 1.0 cm2 with thickness of about 323.7-325.2
mg/cm2. Different sets of Al-wrapped 232Th samples
The lithium foil used was made up of natural
lithium with thickness of 3.7 mg/cm2, sandwiched
between two tantalum foils. The front tantalum foil
is 3.9 mg/cm2 thick, in which the degradation of
proton energy is only 50 to 80 keV and the back
tantalum foil is 0.025 to 0.1 mm thick. Different
sets of Ta-Li-Ta stacks were made.
80 cm3 HPGe detector coupled to a PC-based 4K
channel analyzer was used for the gamma-rays counting
of the fission products. The resolution of the
detector system during counting was 1.8 keV FWHM at
233 minus average light and heavy fragment mass
